title: image
text: image Uniface data type containing binary data. image ParameterName : Direction image VariableName Use Use in params and variables blocks to define the data type of a parameter or variable. The data type can be selected when defining fields, global variables, and component variables. Description Use the image data type for fields that contain images from the database, glyphs, disk files, or third-party filters. No arithmetic operations can be applied to this kind of data. It is not possible to directly compare two parameters, variables, or fields that use the data type raw or image —they will always evaluate to True . An alternative is to calculate and compare a hash of the data. Related Links Image Handling Uniface can display and print pictures that are stored as image files on disk, as glyphs, or BLOBs in a database. The type of picture can affect performance. Data Handling in ProcScript The ProcScript language enables you to convert data from one type to another, perform arithmetic operations, extract data values, and perform string substitution. Uniface carries out implicit type conversion whenever necessary, for example, when assignments are made between non-matching data types or when comparing non-matching data types. The ProcScript language also provides a number of functions that allow you to convert from one data type to another. Packing Codes for Other Data String, Image, and Raw data can be mapped to a variety of packing codes, including W for Unicode characters. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
